title: Maintenance / versioning / errata + regeneration process
context: As the dataset library grows, datasets will need corrections (errata), source-calibration updates, and version bumps. Without a documented maintenance process, corrections are ad-hoc and unreproducible. Synthetic datasets must regenerate deterministically from their committed seed/params (same seed => identical bytes after a fix), so every corrected version is independently verifiable. The maintenance process must define: how errata are issued, how version bumps work (semver), how reproducibility checks are re-run on each release, and what triggers a dataset to become a maintenance task. Stale/incorrect datasets should surface as maintenance tasks in the backlog. Depends on zenodo-020 (versioning automation) and tooling-006 (validation).
objective: Document the maintenance, versioning, errata, and regeneration process for the dataset library, ensuring every corrected version is reproducibly regenerated and every release re-passes the reproducibility check.
acceptanceCriteria: Documented process to issue corrected versions: errata file format, version bump requirements (semver patch/minor/major), and communication to adopters.
Synthetic datasets regenerate deterministically from recorded seed/params: documented how a corrected parameter file leads to an identically-reproducible new version.
Reproducibility check re-run requirement: every release (including errata) must re-pass the reproducibility check (same seed => identical bytes) before Zenodo DOI update.
Process for flagging stale/incorrect datasets as maintenance tasks in the project backlog.
Named steward (or role) responsible for ongoing adoption liaison and dataset maintenance identified.
Document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off.
output: A maintenance/versioning/errata/regeneration process document committed to the project repo, covering errata format, semver bumps, reproducibility re-check, and named steward.
